sources.issuing_organisation: American College of Cardiology, American Heart Association, and Heart Rhythm Society
sources.exact_document_title: 2018 Guideline on the Evaluation and Management of Patients With Bradycardia and Cardiac Conduction Delay: Guidelines Made Simple
sources.version: 2018 ACC/AHA/HRS Guidelines Made Simple selection of tables and figures
sources.population: Patients with symptoms suggestive of or consistent with bradycardia or cardiac conduction disease.
sources.clinical_setting: Initial, outpatient, acute, and specialist evaluation of suspected or documented bradycardia and conduction disorders.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-led history, physical examination, ECG and selected test review, symptom-rhythm correlation, reversible-cause context, and monitor selection. It is old, is a condensed official summary, and does not authorize automatic rhythm diagnosis, pacing, treatment, or referral.

exact_sections.heading: Evaluation of Bradycardia and Conduction Disease Algorithm
exact_sections.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF page 9, Figure 1, plus page 10 reversible-cause table
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports comprehensive clinician history and physical examination, ECG, directed blood testing, symptom frequency, selected ambulatory monitoring, and reversible or treatable cause context without generating a diagnosis or test order.

exact_sections.heading: Chronic bradycardia and conduction-disorder algorithms
exact_sections.locator: Guidelines Made Simple PDF pages 11-13 and 23-24
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting symptom-rhythm correlation, symptom frequency, structural-disease context, reviewed ECG or ambulatory monitoring, and observed follow-up when appropriate; pacing and treatment branches are not mapped.
